confidentiality_scope: Recipient shall hold in confidence all information disclosed by Disclosing Party, whether or not marked Confidential, that a reasonable person would deem confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.
Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care.

Confidential Information includes all written, oral, and electronic information disclosed in connection with the Purpose, except information that is publicly available through no fault of the receiving party.
term_duration: Confidentiality obligations shall survive for five (5) years following termination of this Agreement.
Confidentiality obligations shall survive for three (3) years following termination, except trade secrets which survive indefinitely.
The term of this Agreement is two (2) years; confidentiality obligations survive for the longer of five (5) years or until information enters the public domain.
return_of_materials: Upon termination, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Materials within thirty (30) days and certify destruction in writing.
Each party shall, upon written request, return or destroy the other party's Confidential Materials within fifteen (15) business days.
Recipient may retain one archival copy solely for compliance purposes, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.
governing_law: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California, excluding its conflict-of-laws rules.
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York; parties consent to exclusive jurisdiction in New York County courts.
remedies: Disclosing Party may seek injunctive relief without posting bond for actual or threatened breach of confidentiality obligations.
The parties acknowledge that breach may cause irreparable harm; either party may seek equitable relief in addition to monetary damages.
Remedies are cumulative; failure to exercise a remedy shall not constitute a waiver of future enforcement.
indemnification: Each party shall indemnify the other only for direct damages caused by its gross negligence or willful misconduct, capped at $500,000 aggregate.
Recipient's indemnification obligation is limited to direct damages arising from unauthorized disclosure caused by Recipient's breach, capped at fees paid in the prior twelve (12) months.
Indemnification excludes consequential damages and applies only to third-party claims, with prompt notice and control of defense cooperation.
scope_of_services: Provider shall perform services described in each Statement of Work executed by both parties, incorporating mutually agreed milestones and acceptance criteria.
Services include implementation, configuration, and support as specified in Exhibit A; out-of-scope work requires a change order signed by both parties.
Provider shall assign qualified personnel and maintain service levels set forth in the applicable SOW; Client shall provide timely access and approvals.
intellectual_property: Client owns all deliverables created specifically for Client under paid SOWs; Provider retains Background IP and grants Client a perpetual license to embedded Background IP in deliverables.
Work product is works-made-for-hire; to the extent not assignable by law, Provider assigns all rights upon payment of applicable fees.
Provider retains platform IP; Client receives a non-exclusive license during the term. Custom integrations developed for Client are owned by Client upon full payment.
data_security: Provider shall maintain SOC 2 Type II controls, encrypt data in transit and at rest, and notify Client within twenty-four (24) hours of a confirmed security incident.
Provider shall implement administrative, technical, and physical safeguards consistent with industry standards and applicable data protection laws.
Provider shall not use Client data to train models or improve services without Client's prior written consent; subprocessors require equivalent obligations.
limitation_of_liability: Each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed the greater of fees paid in twelve (12) months or $1,000,000, excluding gross negligence, willful misconduct, and IP infringement.
Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages; direct damages capped at fees paid in the six (6) months preceding the claim.
Liability cap does not apply to breaches of confidentiality, data security obligations, or either party's indemnification obligations for third-party IP claims.
dispute_resolution: Parties shall escalate disputes to executives for good-faith negotiation; unresolved disputes submitted to JAMS arbitration in San Francisco under expedited procedures.
Disputes shall be resolved by litigation in state or federal courts of competent jurisdiction in the county of the defendant's principal place of business.
Parties agree to mediate in good faith before arbitration; prevailing party entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees for IP and confidentiality claims.
termination: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach on thirty (30) days written notice, or for convenience on ninety (90) days notice with pro-rata fee refund.
Client may terminate for convenience on sixty (60) days notice; Provider may terminate only for non-payment exceeding thirty (30) days.
Upon termination, Provider shall provide data export in standard format within thirty (30) days and certify deletion of Client data.
severance_payment: Employer shall pay nine (9) months base salary plus pro-rata target bonus, subject to signed release and compliance with restrictive covenants.
Severance equals six (6) months salary payable in installments aligned with payroll schedule, less applicable withholdings.
Severance conditioned on Employee's return of company property and non-revocation of ADEA release during the seven (7) day period.
non_compete: Employee agrees not to solicit Employer's customers or employees for twelve (12) months within the territory where Employee worked.
Non-compete limited to direct competition with Employer's core product line for six (6) months in counties where Employee had material contact.
Restrictive covenants enforceable only to the extent permitted by applicable law; if overbroad, courts may blue-pencil to minimum necessary scope.
ip_assignment: Employee assigns inventions made during employment relating to Employer's business, excluding inventions listed on Prior Inventions Schedule.
Assignment covers work product created using Employer resources or relating to Employer's Confidential Information during employment.
Employee shall assist in securing IP rights at Employer's expense during and after employment for assigned inventions.
release_of_claims: Employee releases all claims through Separation Date under federal and state law, excluding workers' compensation and vested benefits.
Release includes ADEA waiver with twenty-one (21) day consideration period and seven (7) day revocation for employees age 40+.
Employee does not release claims arising after signing or rights that cannot be waived as a matter of law.
purchase_price: Purchase price adjusted to $12,600,000 with seller credit of $150,000 for deferred maintenance items identified in inspection report.
Price remains $12,750,000 with buyer assuming responsibility for roof repairs capped at $75,000 via escrow holdback.
Final price subject to proration of taxes, rent, and CAM charges as of closing date per standard closing statement.
due_diligence: Inspection period extended ten (10) days for completion of Phase I ESA and structural engineer report at buyer's expense.
Buyer may terminate during inspection period for any reason with return of earnest money; seller to provide access and existing reports.
Due diligence items limited to environmental, title, and survey; buyer waives further inspection rights after expiration.
as_is_condition: Property sold as-is except for seller's representations regarding title, authority, and known latent defects listed in Schedule B.
Buyer accepts property condition based on inspections; seller makes no warranties regarding habitability or fitness for particular purpose.
As-is sale with seller responsible for remediation of pre-closing environmental violations identified in Phase I ESA.
confidentiality: Employee shall maintain confidentiality of Employer's trade secrets, customer lists, and proprietary business information indefinitely, except as required by law or with Employer's written consent.
Employee agrees not to disclose or use Confidential Information for any purpose other than performing employment duties, during employment and for three (3) years following separation.

Confidentiality obligations survive termination without temporal limit as to information qualifying as trade secrets under Cal. Civ. Code § 3426; other confidential information protected for five (5) years post-separation.
service_levels: Provider shall maintain 99.9% monthly uptime for production Services, excluding scheduled maintenance windows of up to four (4) hours per month with 72-hour advance notice. Service credits equal 5% of monthly fees for each 0.1% below target.
Provider guarantees API response times at p95 ≤ 500ms and assigns a dedicated support engineer for Severity 1 incidents with one-hour response and four-hour workaround targets.
SLA credits capped at 25% of monthly fees; chronic failures (three consecutive months below target) entitle Client to terminate without penalty and receive pro-rata refund of prepaid fees.
audit_rights: Client may audit Provider's SOC 2 Type II controls and data processing practices once annually on thirty (30) days notice, at Client's expense, with findings remediated within sixty (60) days.

Provider shall make available audit reports from independent third-party assessors upon request under NDA; on-site audits limited to once per year unless a security incident triggers extraordinary audit rights.
Audit scope includes subprocessors handling Client personal data; Provider shall notify Client of new subprocessors thirty (30) days before engagement and allow objection on reasonable grounds.
